rationale:
Both runs ship a functionally complete, dependency-free notes API through src/server.mjs honoring PORT, with all four endpoints, file persistence, and JSON error paths. Both pass the objective checks (tests + probe-http-lifecycle) and the baseline fixture stays green; neither adds npm deps and neither is a false pass.

Run A is stronger on every dimension. Tests: 10 requirement-mapped tests (separate create/list/fetch/delete, a real two-process restart-persistence test, dedicated 404/400 cases, and a child-process CLI test honoring PORT) vs Run B's 3 coarse tests (one bundles all CRUD). Evaluation trustworthiness is the biggest gap: Run A's evaluator independently exercised the live API with saved curl transcripts showing concrete 201/200/400/404/405 responses, a genuine restart transcript with port-closed verification, AND discovered and reproduced a real (low-severity, out-of-contract) write-queue-poisoning bug with saved stderr — 4 frozen evidence files, high confidence/strong evidence. Run B's evaluator did code review plus a single rerun of `npm test` (1 frozen log), self-admits no live API session, and rates medium/adequate; individual error-path status codes are not separately evidenced.

Harness process: Run A ran clean in one generator pass. Run B hit provider usage limits twice and needed two restarts over ~2.5h; resume worked correctly (a credit to the harness) but the process was noisier. Both used the same minimal ceremony level, so contract/planning artifacts are identical and generic.

The only defect in Run A is a low-severity robustness edge case outside the contracted behavior, fully documented. Run A wins clearly on product quality and decisively on evidence quality.
